steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The underlined portion is a simile: "like a flock of crows on seed potatoes." The question asks what function this simile serves in the passage as a whole.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It discusses an event that Mrs. Tarpey believes is unlikely to occur.': Mrs. Tarpey says nothing about crows or an unlikely event — the simile belongs to Bartley's speech. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It presents a vivid image to emphasize a claim that Bartley makes.': Bartley claims misfortune always falls on him; the simile of crows descending on seed potatoes vividly reinforces that claim. ✓
  • Choice C — 'It mounts a counterargument to a claim that Bartley made about the fair.': The simile continues and intensifies Bartley's complaint — it does not argue against it. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It describes a scene that Bartley witnessed at the fair.': The simile is figurative language about his misfortune, not a literal scene Bartley saw. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
